title: Capability-grant infrastructure (FEAT-004/005 + DEC-0047 + TASK-089/092) operates at orchestrator-tier but is no-op at agent-dispatch tier — grants are computed and clamped at jit-runtime.ts line 488 then discarded with the declared tools; closure tracked by multi-tool dispatch loop implementation per FGAP-169
description: The capability-grant infrastructure landed across multiple completed substrate items provides no value at agent dispatch. FEAT-004 (composeToolGrant) computes parent-grant intersection with requested-grant. FEAT-005 (capability composition) commits to 'subagent receives its OWN tools, JIT-composed per invocation, scoped to EXACTLY the operations the task needs.' DEC-0047 enacts the capability-grant model: spec-declared, default-empty, operation-granular, clamped at dispatch. TASK-089 delivered composeToolGrant + 55-operation vocabulary. TASK-092 delivered Hybrid-3-v2 composite-tool infrastructure (per-KIND triples + 5-layer forbidden-wholesale + author-tool-grant). The 6 static + dynamic composite Pi tools (call-agent, author-agent-spec, run-real-checks, commit-attested, author-tool-grant, run-work-order-loop + per-config.tool_operations[] composites) exist and operate at the orchestrator-tier — orchestrator-LLM in pi REPL receives granted composite tools and exercises them. At the AGENT DISPATCH tier (call-agent dispatching a subagent), executeAgent at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/jit-runtime.ts line 488 calls computeGrantViolation(compiled.tools, dispatch.parentGrant) for the grant clamp; on pass, line 568 then registers ONLY the framework's phantom-tool (when outputSchema is set). The declared tools and their associated grant are not delivered to the LLM. Per FGAP-169: the capability-grant model logically requires that granted capabilities then EXIST as agent-accessible tools; the current implementation clamps then discards. The infrastructure operates correctly as a build-time gate and provides no runtime value at agent dispatch.

evidence.reference: Single use of compiled.tools + dispatch.parentGrant in the dispatch path: computeGrantViolation clamp check; throws GrantViolationError on subset-violation; on pass, tools + grant are not referenced again. The grant has done its only job at this point.
context.tools = [phantomTool] — the LLM receives ONLY the framework's phantom-tool, gated on if(compiled.outputSchema). The capability-granted declared tools are absent from the LLM's tool registry.

FEAT-004 status proposed/completed (composeToolGrant); FEAT-005 status proposed (capability composition: 'subagent receives its OWN tools, JIT-composed per invocation, scoped to EXACTLY the operations the task needs'). The 'subagent receives its OWN tools' commitment is unmet at the dispatch boundary.

DEC-0047 enacted (capability-grant model: spec-declared, default-empty, operation-granular, clamped at dispatch). The clamp at dispatch (line 488) executes; the granted capabilities are then discarded (not delivered to LLM).

TASK-089 completed 2026-05-27 (pi-agent-dispatch extension; FEAT-004/005 composeToolGrant + operation-vocab 55 names). TASK-092 completed 2026-05-29 (FEAT-010 Hybrid-3-v2 composite-tool infrastructure + author-tool-grant). Both shipped infrastructure that operates correctly at build/grant-time but provides no value at agent runtime.

FGAP-169 sharpened body documents the implementation-level fact (line 488/568 wiring); FGAP-177 documents the substrate-contradiction (Body A vs Body B). FGAP-178 (this) names the operational consequence: the capability-grant infrastructure is no-op at runtime.

impact: The engineering invested in FEAT-004/005, DEC-0047, TASK-089, TASK-092 (composeToolGrant + 55-operation vocabulary + Hybrid-3-v2 composite-tool infrastructure + 5-layer forbidden-wholesale clamp + author-tool-grant Pi tool + per-config.tool_operations[] dynamic composites + operation-granular grant-composition model) produces value at the orchestrator-tier but no value at the subagent-dispatch tier while the implementation strips declared tools at line 488 and never delivers them to the LLM. The author-tool-grant Pi tool authoring affordance lets operators compose grants for subagent dispatch that no subagent can exercise. The orchestrator-tier dispatch (scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh granting composite tools to the orchestrator-LLM) operates correctly per the substrate. The subagent-tier dispatch (call-agent invoking executeAgent with declared tools[]) is the gap.
canonical_vocabulary: infrastructure dormancy; no-op capability semantics; agent-tier vs orchestrator-tier divergence
proposed_resolution: Closure tracked by Phase-3 of the canon-alignment arc: multi-tool dispatch loop in executeAgent per FGAP-169 proposed_resolution. The loop wires compiled.tools (intersected with dispatch.parentGrant) into the LLM's tool registry; the subagent dispatches its granted tools through ExtensionContext; the composeToolGrant + author-tool-grant + operation-vocabulary + composite-tool infrastructure becomes load-bearing at agent runtime. Verification: the existing composite tools (read-real-checks, commit-attested, run-work-order-loop, dynamic per-config composites) become invocable by subagents under their grants, not only by orchestrator-LLMs.
